records.ety: [OE. jornee, journee, prop., a day's journey, OF. jorne, jurne, a day, a day's work of journey, F. journe, fr. OF. jorn, jurn, jor a day, F. jour, fr. L. diurnus. See Journal.]
usage: -- Journey, Tour, Excursion, Pilgrimage. The word journey suggests the idea of a somewhat prolonged traveling for a specific object, leading a person to pass directly from one point to another. In a tour, we take a roundabout course from place to place, more commonly for pleasure, though sometimes on business. An excursion is usually a brief tour or trip for pleasure, health, etc. In a pilgrimage we travel to a place hallowed by our religions affections, or by some train of sacred or tender associations. A journey on important business; the tour of Europe; an excursion to the lakes; a pilgrimage to the Holy Land.

defs: The travel or work of a day.
quotes: We have yet large day, for scarce the sun Hath finished half his journey.

defs: Travel or passage from one place to another, especially one covering a large distance or taking a long time.
quotes: The good man . . . is gone a long journey.

defs: A passage through life, or a passage through any significant experience, or from one state to another.
quotes: We must all have the same journey's end.

defs: The distance that is traveled in a journey{2}, or the time taken to complete a journey{2}; as, it's a two-day journey from the oasis into Cairo by camel; from Mecca to Samarkand is quite a journey.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Journeyed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Journeying.]

defs: To travel from place to place; to go from home to a distance.
quotes: Abram journeyed, going on still toward the south.

defs: To traverse; to travel over or through.
